ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general green tea beverage class; no specific plum-flavoured less-sugar variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific plum green tea subcode exists. Approximate match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for plum-flavoured green tea beverage; left null to avoid fabrication.

preparation_method: chilled or served at room temperature

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of plum green tea (less sugar) is approximately 350 ml (350 g), providing about 151 kcal and 28.9 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Plum Green Tea (Less Sugar) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Plum Green Tea (Less Sugar), CR ID D08010093. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for plum green tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 52 based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages with comparable sugar content (~8 g/100 g) from the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 52 falls within the low GI range (≤55) per the University of Sydney classification standard. Glycemic load per 350 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (52 × 22.9) / 100 ≈ 11.9, using sugar fraction (8.26 g/100 g × 3.5 = 28.91 g; net available carbohydrate adjusted to 22.9 g using the sugar as primary glycaemic driver). Estimation basis documented per Veritas policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar content 8.26 g/100 g in a beverage — classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) per AHA criteria; SSB consumption independently elevates blood pressure risk regardless of low sodium content.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage and choose genuinely unsweetened green tea, as the 8.26 g/100 g sugar load qualifies it as an SSB that independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, despite its low sodium of 1 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 8.26 g/100 g entirely from free sugars in liquid form with negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g); liquid sugar is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage because its 8.26 g/100 g free-sugar content in liquid form causes rapid blood glucose elevation with no fibre buffering; a 350 ml serving delivers approximately 28.9 g of sugar, well exceeding the ADA recommended limit for sugar-sweetened beverages.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular SSB consumption at this sugar level (8.26 g/100 g) is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and heightened CVD risk per prospective cohort evidence.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or eliminate this beverage, as habitual consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at ≥8 g sugar/100 g is linked to elevated triglycerides and increased CVD incidence; switching to plain unsweetened green tea preserves any polyphenol benefit without the sugar burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Free sugar at 8.26 g/100 g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; the green tea polyphenol (EGCG) concentration in this commercial sweetened beverage is insufficient to confer meaningful anti-inflammatory benefit.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should choose unsweetened green tea to obtain EGCG anti-inflammatory benefits without the pro-inflammatory sugar load present in this less-sugar variant.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plum Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a commercially prepared Singaporean speciality tea beverage consisting of freshly brewed green tea infused with plum flavouring and formulated with a reduced sugar level compared to the standard variant. It is typically served chilled or at room temperature in takeaway cups of approximately 350 ml and may be customised with toppings such as pearls or jelly. The flavour profile is mildly sweet with a tangy plum note layered over the grassy, slightly astringent character of green tea. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage contains 8.26 g of sugar per 100 g — entirely from free sugars — which still classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under AHA and ADA guidelines. A standard 350 ml serving delivers approximately 151 kcal and 28.9 g of sugar with negligible dietary fibre (0.03 g/100 g), meaning the sugar is absorbed rapidly and causes meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation. Sodium content is very low at 1 mg/100 g. The beverage is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its SSB classification. Plain unsweetened green tea is the clinically preferred alternative for those seeking polyphenol (EGCG) benefits without the glycaemic burden. Nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D08010093, confidence 0.95).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with an exact match to CR ID D08010093 at confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which slightly reduces overall confidence but does not affect the high rating for nutrient data.
notes: fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (D08010093); this is a tea-based beverage with no fat-containing ingredients. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero by formulation — no fat source present. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this commercial plum green tea beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D08010093 as of 2026-04-16. GI value of 52 is an analogous estimate based on sugar-sweetened tea beverages with comparable sugar content (~8 g/100 g) from the University of Sydney GI Database; GI 52 is classified as LOW (≤55) per the University of Sydney standard classification; gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' in iteration 3. No direct GI measurement for plum green tea (less sugar) was found in any approved source. The 'less sugar' designation does not confer diabetes-safe or hypertension-safe status under AHA or ADA clinical guidelines. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code for plum-flavoured less-sugar green tea exists as of 2026-04-16.
